sanskrit_devanagari:
यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः ।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥ २३ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
yadi hy ahaṁ na varteyaṁ
jātu karmaṇy atandritaḥ |
mama vartmānuvartante
manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ || 23 ||

anvaya.meaning: in the performance of prescribed duties

translations.verbatim_quote: For if I ever failed to engage in carefully performing prescribed duties, O Pārtha, certainly all men would follow My path.
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: In order to keep the balance of social tranquillity for progress in spiritual life, there are traditional family usages meant for every civilized man. Although such rules and regulations are for the conditioned souls and not Lord Kṛṣṇa, because He descended to establish the principles of religion He followed the prescr

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.23 Again, O Partha, yadi, if; jatu, at any time; aham, I; an, do not; varteyam, continue; atandritah, vigilantly, untiringly; karmani, in action; manusyah, men: anuvartante, willl follow; mama, My; vartma, path; sarvasah, in every way, I being the Highest.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: yadi hy ahaṁ na varteyam — if I were not to remain engaged — jātu — at any time at all — atandritaḥ — without slackness, with full attention — then mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ — men follow my path. The verse therefore extends 3.22 by inserting the quality-of-engagement variable: it is not enough that the śreṣṭha appear to be acting; he must be acting atandritaḥ. Slackness is a form of inaction the populace registers.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.23 If I, the Lord of all, whose will is always true, whose sport consists in creation, sustentation and dissolution of universe at My will, even though I am born at My pleasure as a man to help the world — if, I thus incarnating in the family of Vasudeva who is the foremost amon
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: even the Lord, who has no purpose to serve through action, must act atandritaḥ — without slack, without negligence — because the entire chain of obligation rests on the people patterning themselves on the Lord's visible observance. If the Lord were to act mechanically or with detached half-attention, the people would copy that quality, and the order would degrade.

positions.rendering: if I should ever fail to engage in carefully performing prescribed duties... all men would follow My path in every respect
if I were not to perform prescribed actions diligently... all men would follow My path
atandritaḥ — without slackness; the verse extends the example by making the slackness of even the supreme actor catastrophic for the world's order
if even the Lord were to act without diligence, the entire chain of obligation would collapse, since the people pattern themselves on his observance

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: The translators preserve atandritaḥ as 'carefully' or 'diligently' but flatten its specific function — it is not just adverbial decoration. Both commentators emphasise that the verse is not just naming the consequence of inaction but the consequence of slack action, slack-of-attention action, action performed mechanically. The śreṣṭha's slackness is, in the verse's logic, almost as damaging as his absence.

literal_meaning: For if at any time I were not to engage diligently in action, men would, O Pārtha, follow my path in every way.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna extends his proof-by-example from 3.22 with the consequence-clause: if HE were to step away from action, even briefly, even given his exemption from purpose-deficit, the populace — watching him as the ultimate śreṣṭha — would step away with him. The verse names mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ (men follow my path) and atandritaḥ (without slack, without slackness, diligently) — making explicit that the relevant variable is not just whether he acts but the quality of his action.
